action.reasoning: Observation: the ensemble of stage1 + best stage2 underperformed badly on both local val (0.779) and hidden eval (0.7663), so those checkpoints are not complementary enough for naive averaging. Analysis: our best score remains the calibrated decoupled checkpoint from iteration 6 at 0.8092. The safest path is to preserve that checkpoint and search for a modest gain via test-time augmentation on the single best model, which often helps ConvNeXt with little risk and no retraining. We can implement horizontal-flip TTA in predict.py, verify on val using the existing best checkpoint, and only submit if local val improves over the single-model baseline; otherwise we should revert and keep the known-best submission. Decision: patch predict.py to support optional flip TTA and a local validation mode, measure val balanced accuracy for checkpoints/best.pt with and without TTA, and if TTA helps submit the same checkpoint using the updated predict.py.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output pred_smoke.json --report_val && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output pred_smoke.json --report_val --tta
